title: Aapka Jeera Rice Bhi Chipakta Hai? Yeh Secret Dekho | How to Make Perfect Jeera Rice | Kunal Kapur
description: Jeera rice doesn’t always need a gas stove to come together -you can make it just as easily in the microwave, which is a great option when you’re low on LPG, travelling, or simply want a quicker, fuss-free method. It’s efficient, uses minimal setup, and still gives you that same comforting, aromatic bowl without standing in the kitchen. Perfect for days when you want something simple, reliable, and stress-free. Ingredients: Tempering Time - 4 mins | Power - 100
ingredients: Time - 4 mins | Power - 100 | Oil (तेल) - 1 tbsp | Black cardamom (काली इलाइची) - 2 nos | Bay leaves (तेज पत्ता) - 2 nos | Cumin (जीरा) - ½ tsp | Salt (नमक) - a large pinch | Water (पानी) - 1 cup | Time - 10 mins | Microwave Power - Max | Basmati Rice (चावल) - ½ cup | Salt (नमक) - a pinch (optional) | Water (पानी) - 1 cup

title: Instant Green Chilli Pickle | 5 Min में मिर्च का अचार | Kunal Kapur Recipes
description: Craving something spicy, tangy, and full of flavour? This instant hari mirch ka chaat (green chilli pickle) is your new go-to! Made in just 5 minutes, this recipe needs no cooking, no vinegar, and no sunlight - perfect for when you want a quick desi condiment without the wait. It’s bold, zesty, and pairs beautifully with parathas, dal-chawal, khichdi, or even simple roti-sabzi. If you love traditional Indian pickles but don’t have the time for long processes, this one’s a total game-changer. Instant Green Chilli Pickle Ingredients
ingredients: Green chilli (हरी मिर्च) - 250 gms | Salt (नमक) - 1½ tsp | Fenugreek seeds (मेथी दाना) - 1 tsp | Kalonji (कलौंजी)- 1 tsp | Fennel (सौंफ) - 1 tbsp | Mustard seeds (राई) - 2 tbsp | Asafoetida (हींग) - ½ tsp | Turmeric powder (हल्दी) - 1 tsp | Kashmiri red chilli (कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च) - 1½ tsp | Salt (नमक) - 1½ tsp | Mustard oil (सरसों का तेल) - ¼ cup | Vinegar (सिरका) - 3 tbsp
instructions: Wash the green chillies thoroughly and dry them completely (no moisture). | Cut them into halves or small pieces, depending on your preference. | In a mortar-pestle or mixer (pulse mode), coarsely crush: | fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, mustard seeds, and kalonji. | (Don’t make a fine powder—keep it slightly coarse for texture.) | Heat the mustard oil until it reaches smoking point. | Turn off the flame and let it cool down slightly. | Mix everything together | In a large bowl, add the chopped chillies. | Add salt, turmeric, Kashmiri red chilli powder, hing, and the crushed spices. | Mix well so the chillies are evenly coated. | Add oil & vinegar | Pour the slightly cooled mustard oil over the mixture. | Add vinegar and mix everything really well. | Rest (optional but recommended) | Let it sit for 10–15 minutes so the flavours come together. | 01:10 - Made in just 5 minutes, this recipe needs no cooking, no vinegar, and no sunlight - perfect for when you want a quick desi condiment without the wait. It’s bold, zesty, and pairs beautifully with parathas, dal-chawal, khichdi, or even simple roti-sabzi. If you love traditional Indian pickles but don’t have the time for long processes, this one’s a total game-changer. Instant Green Chilli Pickle Ingredients Green chilli (हरी मिर्च) - 250 gms Salt (नमक) - 1½ tsp Fenugreek seeds (मेथी दाना) - 1 tsp Kalonji (कलौंजी)- 1 tsp Fennel (सौंफ) - 1 tbsp Mustard seeds (राई) - 2 tbsp Asafoetida (हींग) - ½ tsp Turmeric powder (हल्दी) - 1 tsp Kashmiri red chilli (कश्मीरी लाल मिर्च) - 1½ tsp Salt (नमक) - 1½ tsp Mustard oil (सरसों का तेल) - ¼ cup Vinegar (सिरका) - 3 tbsp Method: Wash the green chillies thoroughly and dry them completely (no moisture). Cut them into halves or small pieces, depending on your preference. In a mortar-pestle or mixer (pulse mode), coarsely crush: fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, mustard seeds, and kalonji. (Don’t make a fine powder—keep it slightly coarse for texture.) Heat the mustard oil until it reaches smoking point. Turn off the flame and let it cool down slightly. Mix everything together In a large bowl, add the chopped chillies. Add salt, turmeric, Kashmiri red chilli powder, hing, and the crushed spices. Mix well so the chillies are evenly coated. Add oil & vinegar Pour the slightly cooled mustard oil over the mixture. Add vinegar and mix everything really well. Rest (optional but recommended) Let it sit for 10–15 minutes so the flavours come together. 01:10 - how to slice your green chillies for achaar 03:02 - make the achaar mix 07:34 - how to store your achaar (kunal kapur, instant green chilli pickle, hari mirch ka achar instant, hari mirch ka chaat recipe, green chilli pickle no cook, no cook achar recipe, instant achar without sunlight, no vinegar pickle recipe, 5 minute pickle recipe, quick indian pickle, hari mirch ka instant achar, green chilli chutney style achar, easy achar recipe at home, indian pickle without oil cooking, instant mirchi ka achar, spicy green chilli pickle, homemade achar recipe quick, lunchbox pickle recipe, small batch pickle recipe, quick side dish indian, desi pickle instant, no sun pickle recipe, no fermentation pickle, fresh achar recipe, hari mirch ki launji instant, mirchi ka chatpata achar, indian condiments quick recipe, 5 min recipes indian, easy spicy side dish, quick accompaniments indian meals, pickle for paratha, dal chawal side dish recipe) Hi, I am Chef Kunal Kapur and this is my space, my channel. title: Dhaniya Paratha Recipe | हरे धनिया का पराठा | Coriander leaves paratha | Kunal Kapur Recipes
description: Dhaniya Paratha is a vibrant, flavor-packed Indian flatbread that brings together the freshness of coriander with the warmth of everyday मसाले. Soft on the inside and lightly crisp on the outside, this paratha is perfect for breakfast, lunch, or even a quick dinner. The combination of atta and besan gives it a hearty texture, while ginger, green chilli, and herbs add a refreshing kick. Served hot with dahi, pickle, or a dollop of butter, it’s simple comfort food that feels both wholesome and indulgent. Ingredients Whole Wheat Flour (आटा) – 1 cup Gram Flour (बेसन) – ¼ cup

raw_text: Dhaniya Paratha is a vibrant, flavor-packed Indian flatbread that brings together the freshness of coriander with the warmth of everyday मसाले. Soft on the inside and lightly crisp on the outside, this paratha is perfect for breakfast, lunch, or even a quick dinner. The combination of atta and besan gives it a hearty texture, while ginger, green chilli, and herbs add a refreshing kick. Served hot with dahi, pickle, or a dollop of butter, it’s simple comfort food that feels both wholesome and indulgent. Ingredients Whole Wheat Flour (आटा) – 1 cup Gram Flour (बेसन) – ¼ cup Cumin (जीरा) – ½ tsp Salt (नमक) – to taste Ginger, chopped (अदरक) – 2 tsp Green chilli, chopped (हरी मिर्च) – 2 nos Onion, chopped (प्याज़) – ¼ cup Turmeric (हल्दी) – ½ tsp Chilli powder (मिर्ची पाउडर) – 1 tsp Chaat masala (चाट मसाला) – 1 tsp Fresh coriander, chopped (ताज़ा धनिया) – 1½ cups Mint (पुदीना) – few sprigs Ghee (घी) – 1 tbsp Water (पानी) – as required Ghee (घी) – for smearing Oil (तेल) – as required Method In a large bowl, combine whole wheat flour, gram flour, cumin, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, and chaat masala. Add chopped ginger, green chillies, onion, fresh coriander, and mint leaves. Mix everything well so the herbs are evenly distributed. Add ghee and start kneading the mixture, gradually adding water to form a soft, smooth dough. Cover and let the dough rest for at least 15–20 minutes. Divide the dough into 4 equal portions (about 110 g each) and shape into pedas. Roll each peda into a paratha using a rolling pin. Heat a tawa and cook the paratha on medium flame, applying ghee or oil on both sides, until golden brown spots appear. Serve hot with curd, pickle, or butter. title: Ye Chhota Sa Snack Itna Crispy Kaise Hai? Laccha Aloo Se Bana Sabse Crispy Snack | Kunal Kapur
description: Crispy Lachha Aloo is hands down one of the yummiest snacks you’ll ever try - thinly shredded potatoes fried to golden perfection, light, crunchy, and absolutely addictive. Whether you’re craving a quick evening snack, something to pair with chai, or just want to indulge in a crispy treat, this recipe never disappoints. The texture is what makes it special - perfectly crisp on the outside with that delicate, melt-in-the-mouth bite inside. Made with simple ingredients and minimal effort, this easy crispy potato snack is perfect for both everyday cravings and when you want to impress guests. If you love street-style flavors and quick homemade recipes, this one’s a must-try. Crispy Potato Ingredients Potato (आलू) - ½ kg
ingredients: Potato (आलू) - ½ kg | Salt (नमक) - to taste | Corn starch (कॉर्नस्टार्च) - 2 tbsp (approx)
instructions: To make crispy laccha aloo, start by cutting potatoes into evenly sized juliennes. | Dip them in cold water for 2-3 minutes. | Then, drain the potatoes, add salt, and leave for 10 minutes. | Next, squeeze out the excess water, add corn starch, and mix well. | Shape the potatoes by swirling them with a fork and drop them into hot oil. | Your crispy laccha aloo is ready to serve. | (Kunal kapur, crispy lachha aloo, lachha aloo recipe, crispy potato snack, easy potato snacks, snack recipes India, Indian snack recipes, evening snacks recipes, tea time snacks, quick snacks at home, easy snacks to make at home, potato snacks recipes, aloo snacks easy, crispy fried potatoes, street style snacks, veg snacks recipes, quick veg snacks, homemade snacks India) | 00:00 - 00:42 - Intro | 00:42 - 01:17 - How to cut potato with a knife | 01:17 - 02:01 - How to cut potato with a mandolin | 02:01 - 03:01 - How to make aloo ke lachhe | 03:03 - 03:56 - Hack to make the crispiest lachha aloo | 04:00 - 04:52 - Final prep before frying | 05:44 - 08-21 - Technique to fry lachhe aloo | Hi, I am Chef Kunal Kapur and this is my space, my channel. | Subscribe & Together let's cook. | 👉🏻 Click here to Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/c/KunalKapur | 👉🏻 Website: http://www.chefkunalkapur.com/​​​​​​​ | Get the knife here: https://vbdace.com/product/chefs-knife

Crispy Potato Ingredients Potato (आलू) - ½ kg Salt (नमक) - to taste Corn starch (कॉर्नस्टार्च) - 2 tbsp (approx) Oil (तेल) - for frying Method To make crispy laccha aloo, start by cutting potatoes into evenly sized juliennes. title: Ashtami Special | Halwa Puri Chana | Kunal Kapur Recipes
description: Ashtami prasad, made with love ✨ Fluffy sooji halwa, perfectly spiced kala chana, and golden puris - each bite full of warmth, tradition, and devotion. Aaj bana mere kitchen main and I’m sharing the most foolproof recipe with you guys - recreate it at home tomorrow, share it with your loved ones, and celebrate the joy of giving, gratitude, and festive flavours. 🌼 Ingredients
ingredients: Kala Chana (boiled) – 2cups | Oil – 3 tbsp | Cumin seeds – 1 tsp | Green chilli – 1no | Ginger chopped – 2tsp | Turmeric powder – ½ tsp | Chilli powder – 1 tsp | Coriander powder – 1 tbsp | Ghee – 1 tbsp | Wheat flour – 2 cups | Salt – ½ tsp | Water – as required | Oil – 1 tbsp | Water – 3cups | Sugar – ¾ cup | Cardamom powder – ½ tsp | Ghee – ⅔ cup | Suji (semolina) – 1cup | Dry fruits chopped – handful
instructions: * Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. | * Add green chilli and ginger, sauté briefly. | * Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. | * Add boiled kala chana, mix well and cook for 5–7 minutes (add a little water if needed). | * Finish with ghee and set aside. | * Mix wheat flour, salt and oil. | * Add water and knead into a stiff dough. | Rest for 10–15 minutes. | * Divide into small balls, roll into discs. | * Deep fry in hot oil till puffed and golden. | * Boil water, sugar and cardamom powder till sugar dissolves (keep warm). | * Heat ghee, add suji and roast till golden and aromatic. | * Add dry fruits, sauté briefly. | * Carefully add warm syrup, stirring continuously. | * Cook till thick and it leaves the sides of the pan. | * Serve hot poori with kala chana and suji halwa ✨ | {kunal kapur, ashtami prasad, navratri recipes, kanjak bhog, sooji halwa, kala chana recipe, puri recipe, navratri food, indian festive food, prasad recipe, ashtami bhog, traditional indian sweets, poori chana halwa, navratri special, sattvik food, indian kitchen, festive cooking, bhog ideas, navratri thali, home cooking, prasad thali, kanjak pooja food, indian desserts} | Hi, I am Chef Kunal Kapur and this is my space, my channel. | Subscribe & Together let's cook. | 👉🏻 Click here to Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/c/KunalKapur | 👉🏻 Website: http://www.chefkunalkapur.com/​​​​​​​ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
raw_text: Ashtami prasad, made with love ✨ Fluffy sooji halwa, perfectly spiced kala chana, and golden puris - each bite full of warmth, tradition, and devotion. Aaj bana mere kitchen main and I’m sharing the most foolproof recipe with you guys - recreate it at home tomorrow, share it with your loved ones, and celebrate the joy of giving, gratitude, and festive flavours. 🌼 Ingredients For Chana: Kala Chana (boiled) – 2cups Oil – 3 tbsp Cumin seeds – 1 tsp Green chilli – 1no Ginger chopped – 2tsp Turmeric powder – ½ tsp Chilli powder – 1 tsp Coriander powder – 1 tbsp Salt Ghee – 1 tbsp For poori: Wheat flour – 2 cups Salt – ½ tsp Water – as required Oil – 1 tbsp Suji Halwa For Syrup: Water – 3cups Sugar – ¾ cup Cardamom powder – ½ tsp For Halwa: Ghee – ⅔ cup Suji (semolina) – 1cup Dry fruits chopped – handful Method Chana * Heat oil, add cumin seeds and let them splutter. * Add green chilli and ginger, sauté briefly. * Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. * Add boiled kala chana, mix well and cook for 5–7 minutes (add a little water if needed). * Finish with ghee and set aside. Poori * Mix wheat flour, salt and oil. * Add water and knead into a stiff dough. Rest for 10–15 minutes. * Divide into small balls, roll into discs. * Deep fry in hot oil till puffed and golden. Halwa * Boil water, sugar and cardamom powder till sugar dissolves (keep warm). * Heat ghee, add suji and roast till golden and aromatic. * Add dry fruits, sauté briefly. * Carefully add warm syrup, stirring continuously. * Cook till thick and it leaves the sides of the pan. Khoya Halwa Ingredients
ingredients: Khoya (खोया) - 500 gm | Water (पानी) - 1 cup | Sugar (चीनी) - 2 tbsp | Sugar (चीनी) - 2 tbsp | Ghee (घी) - 1 tbsp | Cardamom powder (इलाइची पाउडर) - ¾ tsp | Nutmeg powder (जायफल पाउडर) - ½ tsp | Dry fruits, chopped (ड्राई फ्रूट्स) - handful
instructions: Prepare the sugar syrup: | In a pan, add water and sugar. | Let it simmer until the sugar dissolves completely. | Cook the khoya: | Heat ghee in a heavy-bottom pan. | Crumble the khoya and add it to the pan. | Cook on low flame, stirring continuously, until it softens and releases a rich aroma. | Add sugar & syrup: | Add the sugar and prepared sugar syrup to the khoya. | Mix well and continue cooking on low flame. | Cook till thick: | Keep stirring until the mixture thickens and starts leaving the sides of the pan, turning glossy and halwa-like. | Finish with flavour: | Add cardamom powder and nutmeg powder. | Garnish & serve: | Top with chopped dry fruits and serve warm. | If you try this recipe, I’d love to see it - share your pictures or tag me. | Don’t forget to like, share, and subscribe for more simple, delicious recipes. | (kunal kapur, khoya halwa, mawa halwa recipe, indian dessert recipes, quick indian sweets, easy halwa recipe, festive dessert ideas, homemade mithai, khoya recipes, 10 minute dessert, vrat, navratri, falhari, falari, vrat ka khana, fasting, navratri vrat recipes, navratri special recipes, vrat recipes, fasting recipes indian, navratri food ideas, vrat ka khana, navratri sweets recipes, vrat dessert recipes, navratri bhog recipes, vrat ka halwa, halwa recipe indian, navratri halwa recipe, easy halwa recipe, instant halwa recipe, dessert for fasting, satvik sweets recipe, mawa halwa recipe, khoya dessert recipe, milk sweets recipe indian, mawa sweet recipe, quick mithai recipe, easy, 10 minute, instant, vrat friendly, satvik, no onion no garlic) | Hi, I am Chef Kunal Kapur and this is my space, my channel. | Subscribe & Together let's cook. | 👉🏻 Click here to Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/c/KunalKapur | 👉🏻 Website: http://www.chefkunalkapur.com/​​​​​​​ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
raw_text: If you’re craving something rich, comforting, and quick to make, this khoya (mawa) halwa is exactly what you need. It comes together in minutes but tastes like something truly festive and indulgent. Made with just a handful of ingredients, this is one of those classic Indian desserts that never fails - perfect for celebrations, last-minute guests, or simply when you want something sweet without too much effort. Khoya Halwa Ingredients Khoya (खोया) - 500 gm For Sugar Syrup Water (पानी) - 1 cup Sugar (चीनी) - 2 tbsp Sugar (चीनी) - 2 tbsp Ghee (घी) - 1 tbsp Cardamom powder (इलाइची पाउडर) - ¾ tsp Nutmeg powder (जायफल पाउडर) - ½ tsp Dry fruits, chopped (ड्राई फ्रूट्स) - handful Method Prepare the sugar syrup: In a pan, add water and sugar. Let it simmer until the sugar dissolves completely. Keep aside. Cook the khoya: Heat ghee in a heavy-bottom pan. Crumble the khoya and add it to the pan. Cook on low flame, stirring continuously, until it softens and releases a rich aroma. Add sugar & syrup: Add the sugar and prepared sugar syrup to the khoya. Mix well and continue cooking on low flame. Cook till thick: Keep stirring until the mixture thickens and starts leaving the sides of the pan, turning glossy and halwa-like. Finish with flavour: Add cardamom powder and nutmeg powder. Mix well. Garnish & serve: Top with chopped dry fruits and serve warm. title: Appe Recipe | South Indian Appe | Kunal Kapur Recipes
description: Crispy on the outside, soft and cheesy inside - these masala cheese appe are my kind of perfect breakfast / chai-time snack. Light, flavourful, and packed with little bursts of spice, they’re the kind of bite-sized treats that disappear the moment they hit the table. Masala cheese Appe (makes 36 appe) Ingredients Semolina - 1 cup
ingredients: Semolina - 1 cup | Curd - ¾ cup | Salt - 1 tsp (to taste) | Water - 1 cup | Peppercorn - 15 nos | Oil - 2 tbsp | Drty red chillies - 2 nos | Asafoetida - ½ tsp | Mustard seeds - 1 tsp | Cumin - 1 tsp | Channa dal - 2 tsp | Urad dal - 1 tsp | Ginger, chopped - 2 tsp | Green chilli, chopped - 2 nos | Carrot, grated - ½ cup | Onion, chopped - ½ cup | Coriander, chopped - handful | Baking powder/Eno - 1 tsp
instructions: In a bowl, combine semolina, curd, salt and water to make a smooth batter. | Let it rest for 10–15 minutes so the semolina softens. | Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan. | Add peppercorns and dry red chillies and sauté briefly. | Add asafoetida, mustard seeds, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. | Let them crackle and turn lightly golden. | Add chopped ginger, green chilli and curry leaves. | Sauté until fragrant. | Add grated carrot and chopped onion and cook for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened. | Turn off the heat and mix in chopped coriander. | Add this tempering to the rested batter and mix well. | Just before cooking, add baking powder/eno and gently mix. | Heat an appe pan and lightly spray oil in each cavity. | Pour some batter, place a small cube of cheese in the centre (optional), and cover with a little more batter. | Cook on medium heat, turning occasionally, until the appe are golden and crisp on all sides. | Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup. | (Kunal kapur, appe recipe, rava appe recipe, masala appe, cheese appe recipe, South Indian snacks, easy breakfast recipe, tea time snacks, vegetarian snack recipes, appe pan recipes, quick Indian snacks, kuzhi paniyaram, south indian appe, paddu, how to make appe, easy breakfast recipes, how to make breakfast in 10 mins) | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | Hi, I am Chef Kunal Kapur and this is my space, my channel. | Subscribe & Together let's cook. | 👉🏻 Click here to Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/c/KunalKapur | 👉🏻 Website: http://www.chefkunalkapur.com/​​​​​​​ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
raw_text: Crispy on the outside, soft and cheesy inside - these masala cheese appe are my kind of perfect breakfast / chai-time snack. Light, flavourful, and packed with little bursts of spice, they’re the kind of bite-sized treats that disappear the moment they hit the table. Masala cheese Appe (makes 36 appe) Ingredients Semolina - 1 cup Curd - ¾ cup Salt - 1 tsp (to taste) Water - 1 cup Peppercorn - 15 nos Oil - 2 tbsp Drty red chillies - 2 nos Asafoetida - ½ tsp Mustard seeds - 1 tsp Cumin - 1 tsp Channa dal - 2 tsp Urad dal - 1 tsp Ginger, chopped - 2 tsp Green chilli, chopped - 2 nos Curry leaves - a sprig Carrot, grated - ½ cup Onion, chopped - ½ cup Coriander, chopped - handful Baking powder/Eno - 1 tsp Oil - gentle spray Cheese cubes - few (optional) Oil - gentle spray Method 1. In a bowl, combine semolina, curd, salt and water to make a smooth batter. Let it rest for 10–15 minutes so the semolina softens. 2. Heat 2 tbsp oil in a pan. Add peppercorns and dry red chillies and sauté briefly. 3. Add asafoetida, mustard seeds, cumin, chana dal and urad dal. Let them crackle and turn lightly golden. 4. Add chopped ginger, green chilli and curry leaves. Sauté until fragrant. 5. Add grated carrot and chopped onion and cook for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened. Turn off the heat and mix in chopped coriander. 6. Add this tempering to the rested batter and mix well. 7. Just before cooking, add baking powder/eno and gently mix. 8. Heat an appe pan and lightly spray oil in each cavity. Pour some batter, place a small cube of cheese in the centre (optional), and cover with a little more batter. 9. Cook on medium heat, turning occasionally, until the appe are golden and crisp on all sides. 10. Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup. Made by my mom, this used to be my favourite breakfast growing up and now it can be yours too. Try this and it'll become a staple at your home. #ajwainkeparothe #mirchajwainparantha #paratha #parantharecipe #chefkunal #kunalkapur #gharkakhaana ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Mirch Ajwain Paratha Prep time - 10 mins Cooking time - 15 mins Serves - 2 Ingredients For dough Atta - 2 cup Salt Water - ¾ cup For filling Salt Chilli flakes Coriander powder Ajwain Ghee Method: Begin by kneading a medium-soft dough using salt, water, and whole wheat flour. Let the dough rest for 5 minutes. After resting, cut the dough into small portions (pedas), roll them in dry flour, and let them rest for an additional 2 minutes. Then, roll each portion out into discs with light pressure to ensure they spread evenly. Next, spread ghee on top of each disc and sprinkle it with salt, carom seeds, coriander powder, and chili flakes. Shape the discs as demonstrated in the video. Heat some ghee in a tawa (flat pan), and cook the parathas on both sides until they puff up and turn golden brown. title: The BEST Crispy Fried Potato Recipe | Chilli Garlic Dip | Quick Snacks Recipe | Kunal Kapur Recipes
description: आलू को यूंही सब्जियों का राजा नहीं बोलते। जैसी रेसिपी वैसा इसका स्वाद। Aaj ki recipe is for all my aloo lovers, which is gooey from inside and super crispy from outside, accompanied with spicy and delicious Chilli Dip. This recipe is a perfect evening snack, no matter the age; everyone will love it equally kyuki iske taste ki guarantee hai meri! #crispyfriedpotato #friedpotato #potatoballs #chefkunalkapur #kunalkapur #friedpotatoballs #snacks #quicksnacks #potatorecipe
ingredients: Potato, medium (आलू) - ½ kg (4 nos) | Butter (मखन) - 2½ tbsp | Italian seasoning (इटालियन मसाला) - 1 tbsp | All Purpose Flour (मैदा) - 4 tbsp | Salt (नमक) - to taste | Pepper powder (काली मिर्च पाउडर) - ¾ tsp | Spring onion, chopped (हरा प्याज़) - ½ cup | Cornstarch (कोर्न्स्टार्च) - 4 tbsp | Mustard Oil (सरसों तेल) - ¼ cup | Dry Kashmiri Chilli, large (सूखी कश्मीरी मिर्च) - 15-17 nos | Heeng (हींग) - ½ tsp | Coriander seeds (ताज़ा धनिया) - 1 tbsp | Cumin seeds (जीरा) - 2 tsp | Garlic cloves, large (लहसुन) – 12 nos | Lemon, large (नींबू) - 1 nos | Hung curd (गाढ़ी दही) - ½ cup | Salt (नमक) - to taste

raw_text: आलू को यूंही सब्जियों का राजा नहीं बोलते। जैसी रेसिपी वैसा इसका स्वाद। Aaj ki recipe is for all my aloo lovers, which is gooey from inside and super crispy from outside, accompanied with spicy and delicious Chilli Dip. This recipe is a perfect evening snack, no matter the age; everyone will love it equally kyuki iske taste ki guarantee hai meri! #crispyfriedpotato #friedpotato #potatoballs #chefkunalkapur #kunalkapur #friedpotatoballs #snacks #quicksnacks #potatorecipe ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Crispy Fried Potato Balls, Chilli Sauce Prep time - 20 mins Cooking time - 25 mins Serves - 4 Ingredients Potato, medium (आलू) - ½ kg (4 nos) Butter (मखन) - 2½ tbsp Italian seasoning (इटालियन मसाला) - 1 tbsp All Purpose Flour (मैदा) - 4 tbsp Salt (नमक) - to taste Pepper powder (काली मिर्च पाउडर) - ¾ tsp Spring onion, chopped (हरा प्याज़) - ½ cup Cornstarch (कोर्न्स्टार्च) - 4 tbsp Oil (तेल) - for frying Chilli Sauce Mustard Oil (सरसों तेल) - ¼ cup Dry Kashmiri Chilli, large (सूखी कश्मीरी मिर्च) - 15-17 nos Heeng (हींग) - ½ tsp Coriander seeds (ताज़ा धनिया) - 1 tbsp Cumin seeds (जीरा) - 2 tsp Garlic cloves, large (लहसुन) – 12 nos Water (पानी) - a dash Lemon, large (नींबू) - 1 nos Hung curd (गाढ़ी दही) - ½ cup Salt (नमक) - to taste Method Firstly, roughly cut the peeled potatoes into smaller pieces. Next, boil the diced potatoes until they can be easily mashed. Once done, strain them and transfer them to a dry vessel, then mash them thoroughly. In a separate pan, melt some butter and add Italian seasonings along with refined flour. Cook this mixture for about 30 seconds, then add & cook the mashed potatoes until the mixture is completely dry. Spread it out on a deep plate and season with salt, black pepper powder, chili flakes, chopped spring onions, and cornstarch. Knead the mixture well and then form it into small, evenly sized balls. Deep fry the balls until they are golden brown. For the chili dip, heat some mustard oil in a pan and add dried Kashmiri red chilies. Once you remove the chilies, temper the same oil with asafoetida, coriander seeds, cumin seeds, garlic cloves, and roughly chopped ginger. After tempering, grind all these ingredients together with lemon juice and a dash of water, then transfer the mixture to a bowl. In the same bowl, whisk in hung curd and salt, and your chili dip is ready to serve. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Check out other #potatorecipes here: Aloo Ka Soup Kaise Banate Hain: https://youtu.be/a-XNcfEwc0A Shakarkandi/Sweet Potato Chaat: https://youtu.be/wONhHjL_1m8 Korean Fried Potato: https://youtu.be/d4gSynT9Tko Aloo Pyaaz Paratha | Mix Veg Raita: https://youtu.be/r4JUGrrELrw Aloo Methi Paratha: https://youtu.be/17O9LkGfGQA ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- For Complete Recipe - https://www.chefkunalkapur.com/recipe/crispy-fried-potato/ ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Hi, I am Chef Kunal Kapur and this is my space, my channel.
